title: triticum aestivum subsp. inflatum
intro: Triticum aestivum subsp. inflatum, also known as 'inflated wheat', is a subspecies of the common wheat plant, characterized by its inflated spikelets that resemble small balloons. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Triticum aestivum subsp. inflatum include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing individuals to harness its potential health benefits.
